title: Space Tech Briefing — June 04, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts! Welcome to your daily dose of the cosmos, live from the Space Technology Hub on this phenomenal Thursday, June 04, 2026! I'm your host, ready to launch you into the latest and greatest from beyond our blue marble.

As we eagerly anticipate new developments, let's take a moment to look back at some major recent news that's still sending ripples across the space industry. Just a few days ago, the French President made a significant announcement, confirming a groundbreaking agreement with the American company Vast.

This deal sets the stage for two prominent French astronauts, Thomas Pesquet and Arnaud Prost, to embark on distinct private space missions in 2027. Thomas Pesquet, a familiar face from the European Space Agency, is slated to join the International Space Station aboard Vast's very first private mission. Meanwhile, Arnaud Prost is gearing up for a pioneering journey to Vast's brand-new private space station, Haven-1, on its inaugural mission.

This incredible collaboration, unveiled during the "Choose France" summit, is more than just a flight opportunity. It's a strategic move for France, ensuring a continued active presence in low Earth orbit while embracing the future of commercial space by partnering with a trailblazing private space station project. Vast, for its part, is at the forefront of developing what promises to be the first commercial space station, marking a significant step in opening up space to new possibilities. It's a testament to the accelerating pace of private sector involvement in human spaceflight, and we'll be tracking every step of these exciting missions.

That's all for our morning briefing today! Thank you for tuning in and staying connected to the incredible world of space technology. Keep looking up, and we'll talk again tomorrow!
